methodology: Scanner FULL_SIGNAL + multi-source confirmation. Watchlist threshold: 60/100. Conviction threshold: 80/100.

event_driven_candidates.name: Cameco Corporation
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: LEU is the only US-licensed HALEU producer — all US-deployed SMRs will need their fuel. BWXT makes reactor components. Each permit is a forward order for these companies.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Tier-1 uranium mines with 20+ year reserves. Permitting for new uranium mines takes decades — no competitor can build new supply quickly. Cigar Lake is the highest-grade uranium mine operating in the world. As nuclear renaissance accelerates for AI power demand, Cameco is the first call for long-term supply contracts.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Nuclear SMR Accelerated Permitting is reducing the timeline for new reactor deployment. Cameco is already contracted with utilities adding nuclear capacity. AI data center demand for baseload power is the new structural uranium demand driver beyond climate policy.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Nuclear SMR Accelerated Permitting is reducing the timeline for new reactor deployment. Cameco is already contracted with utilities adding nuclear capacity. AI data center demand for baseload power is the new structural uranium demand driver beyond climate policy.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: SMR commercial deployment delayed >3 years from current projections. Uranium spot price drops below $60/lb on inventory destocking. Cigar Lake production disruption. US nuclear reactor retirements accelerate.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: SMR vendor selection and NRC permitting fast-track announcement. New long-term uranium supply contract with a utility. Q2 2026: realized uranium price + contract book update.

event_driven_candidates.name: Vistra Energy
event_driven_candidates.event_name: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: NXT solar trackers are utility-scale — hyperscalers signing direct solar PPAs use NXT hardware. HUBB electrical equipment goes into every data center substation.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Nuclear plants are irreplaceable 40-year assets — cannot build new ones quickly, cannot decommission existing ones without energy crisis. AI data centers are the largest new US electricity demand source. Vistra's nuclear fleet (including Comanche Peak) provides 24/7 carbon-free baseload that data centers specifically want via power purchase agreements. Cannot be replicated.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis — every major hyperscaler is hunting for reliable baseload power. Vistra nuclear fleet is the most direct beneficiary. Microsoft, Google, and Amazon have all signed nuclear PPAs with other utilities; Vistra is the logical next counterparty.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis — every major hyperscaler is hunting for reliable baseload power. Vistra nuclear fleet is the most direct beneficiary. Microsoft, Google, and Amazon have all signed nuclear PPAs with other utilities; Vistra is the logical next counterparty.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Nuclear plant license challenge or unexpected shutdown. Hyperscaler AI infrastructure capex cuts reduce power demand growth. Competing nuclear PPA supply from SMR deployment compresses pricing power.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Nuclear PPA announcement with a hyperscaler (Microsoft, Google, or Amazon). Federal nuclear permitting fast-track decision. Q2 2026 earnings: capacity factor + forward power pricing.

event_driven_candidates.name: Iridium Communications
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: RKLB = direct comp + sentiment lift + launch services demand. IRDM = validated LEO model, M&A target. KTOS = ground infrastructure + defense adjacency. ASTS = direct satellite comms play.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Only truly global satellite constellation — 66 LEO satellites provide pole-to-pole coverage. Maritime, aviation, government, and IoT connectivity markets. Starlink success validates the LEO model and makes IRDM a credible M&A target for hyperscalers or telecom.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: SpaceX Starlink IPO will spotlight the satellite comms sector. IRDM's global coverage moat (poles, oceans) is unique — Starlink cannot serve all IRDM customers without regulatory and hardware changes. M&A premium plausible.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: SpaceX Starlink IPO will spotlight the satellite comms sector. IRDM's global coverage moat (poles, oceans) is unique — Starlink cannot serve all IRDM customers without regulatory and hardware changes. M&A premium plausible.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: SpaceX Starlink achieves polar coverage and directly competes with Iridium's core government/maritime market. Government contract loss — DoD switches primary satcom provider. Revenue growth turns negative.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: SpaceX Starlink IPO filing or direct listing announcement. DoD satcom contract renewal or expansion. Q2 2026 earnings: subscriber growth + government services revenue.

event_driven_candidates.name: Kratos Defense & Security
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: RKLB = direct comp + sentiment lift + launch services demand. IRDM = validated LEO model, M&A target. KTOS = ground infrastructure + defense adjacency. ASTS = direct satellite comms play.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Low-cost autonomous military drone systems — XQ-58 Valkyrie is only production-scale loyal wingman drone in US. Satellite ground systems business provides recurring revenue. Proprietary radar and electronic warfare tech embedded in military programs.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Golden Dome missile defense specifically cites autonomous intercept drones — Kratos Valkyrie is the reference platform. Ukraine conflict validating drone-centric warfare doctrine. Defense budget expansion 2026.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Golden Dome missile defense specifically cites autonomous intercept drones — Kratos Valkyrie is the reference platform. Ukraine conflict validating drone-centric warfare doctrine. Defense budget expansion 2026.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Golden Dome program defunded or significantly restructured. DOD drone acquisition policy shifts to commercial off-the-shelf platforms. Revenue growth below 10% for two consecutive quarters.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Golden Dome contract awards (multiple rounds expected through 2026). Q2 2026 earnings: unmanned systems revenue + classified contract pipeline. Ukraine conflict escalation/de-escalation — direct drone demand signal.

event_driven_candidates.name: Centrus Energy
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: LEU is the only US-licensed HALEU producer — all US-deployed SMRs will need their fuel. BWXT makes reactor components. Each permit is a forward order for these companies.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Only US-licensed enriched uranium producer. Post-Russia sanctions, critical for US energy independence. HALEU (high-assay low-enriched uranium) required for next-gen SMR reactors — Centrus is the only US source.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: SMR reactors require HALEU that only Centrus makes. Post-Russia sanctions creates structural supply gap.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: SMR reactors require HALEU that only Centrus makes. Post-Russia sanctions creates structural supply gap.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Russia sanctions relief restores Rosatom enrichment supply to US utilities. SMR commercial deployment delayed more than 3 years. HALEU demand fails to materialize before DOE funding expires.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: HALEU production contract award from DOE or a commercial SMR developer. SMR NRC permit fast-track announcement. Q2 2026: HALEU production volume + contract pipeline.

event_driven_candidates.name: CACI International
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: KTOS Valkyrie drone is specifically cited in Golden Dome autonomous intercept scenarios. RKLB has space sensor payload capability. Both are still at cyclical troughs.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Classified government contracts for intelligence analysis, cyber operations, and defense IT modernization. Security clearance requirements create near-impenetrable barriers.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Every US military branch is upgrading intelligence and cyber AI capabilities under classified programs. CACI is embedded in programs that cannot be publicly disclosed.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Every US military branch is upgrading intelligence and cyber AI capabilities under classified programs. CACI is embedded in programs that cannot be publicly disclosed.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Material contract loss or protest of a major program. Defense budget sequestration cuts IT modernization before classified AI programs. Revenue growth decelerates below 8% for two quarters.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: contract win rate + revenue growth. Golden Dome or classified AI program contract award announcement. Defense budget continuing resolution — impacts new contract starts.

event_driven_candidates.name: Hubbell
event_driven_candidates.event_name: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: NXT solar trackers are utility-scale — hyperscalers signing direct solar PPAs use NXT hardware. HUBB electrical equipment goes into every data center substation.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Electrical components and grid infrastructure products — transformers, switchgear, wiring devices. Every data center and utility substation uses Hubbell products. Non-discretionary infrastructure.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Grid modernization is a multi-decade program. Every new data center requires Hubbell electrical products — from switchgear to PDUs. AI data center construction boom is a direct demand driver.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Grid modernization is a multi-decade program. Every new data center requires Hubbell electrical products — from switchgear to PDUs. AI data center construction boom is a direct demand driver.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Data center construction starts decline QoQ for two consecutive quarters. Grid modernization budget cuts in IRA reauthorization. Transformer supply chain normalizes and reduces Hubbell's pricing power.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: electrical products order book + data center end-market revenue. Grid Solutions segment growth rate. Any major utility or hyperscaler grid investment announcement.

event_driven_candidates.name: Entegris
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: ENTG supplies advanced materials for every custom ASIC fab process. ONTO does inspection on advanced packaging — every custom chip tape-out uses their tools.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Specialty chemicals, gases, and filtration for leading-edge semiconductor fabs. Consumable — recurring revenue every time a wafer is processed. Critical but invisible in the supply chain.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Wafer starts recovering at TSMC/Samsung. Consumable spend recovers with fab utilization.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Wafer starts recovering at TSMC/Samsung. Consumable spend recovers with fab utilization.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Fab utilization rates fall below 70% — consumable spend contracts sharply. China export control expansion reduces revenue from Chinese fabs. High debt load ($4B+) creates refinancing risk if credit conditions tighten.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Next TSMC monthly revenue data: fab utilization proxy. Q2 2026 earnings: wafer starts volume + advanced node consumable mix. AMAT and LRCX orders as leading indicator.

event_driven_candidates.name: Onto Innovation
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: ENTG supplies advanced materials for every custom ASIC fab process. ONTO does inspection on advanced packaging — every custom chip tape-out uses their tools.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Process control and metrology for advanced packaging (CoWoS, HBM integration). CoWoS is TSMC's packaging technology for NVIDIA GPUs — every HBM stack requires ONTO's inspection systems.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Advanced packaging capex growing rapidly — TSMC expanding CoWoS capacity by 3x for AI GPU demand.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Advanced packaging capex growing rapidly — TSMC expanding CoWoS capacity by 3x for AI GPU demand.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: TSMC CoWoS capacity expansion pauses — reduces ONTO metrology orders. NVIDIA shifts to different packaging architecture that doesn't require ONTO systems. Advanced packaging capex peak before volume ramp.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: TSMC quarterly capex update: CoWoS capacity expansion rate. Q2 2026 earnings: advanced packaging revenue mix + order book. Any NVIDIA next-gen GPU packaging architecture announcement.

event_driven_candidates.name: Eaton Corporation
event_driven_candidates.event_name: AI Data Center Power Demand Crisis
event_driven_candidates.supply_chain_note: NXT solar trackers are utility-scale — hyperscalers signing direct solar PPAs use NXT hardware. HUBB electrical equipment goes into every data center substation.
event_driven_candidates.moat_summary: Industrial switching costs in power management — utilities and data centers run multi-decade procurement relationships with Eaton. Electrical infrastructure (switchgear, PDUs, UPS systems) is spec'd into data center designs years before construction. Near-impossible to displace mid-project.
event_driven_candidates.demand_inflection: Eaton is down 13.5% from its 52-week high while AI data center electrical buildout is accelerating. Every hyperscaler data center requires Eaton power distribution. The company has $14B+ backlog driven by data center orders. Insider bought $390K at trough — recent open-market purchase, not an option exercise.
event_driven_candidates.early_entry_signal: Eaton is down 13.5% from its 52-week high while AI data center electrical buildout is accelerating. Every hyperscaler data center requires Eaton power distribution. The company has $14B+ backlog driven by data center orders. Insider bought $390K at trough — recent open-market purchase, not an option exercise.
event_driven_candidates.invalidation: Hyperscaler capex guidance cut >20% — direct impact on data center electrical buildout. Revenue growth falls below 10% for two consecutive quarters. Backlog growth turns negative.
event_driven_candidates.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (Jul 2026): data center order book size + margin expansion. Any major hyperscaler data center campus announcement is a direct catalyst.

all_entries.name: Taiwan Semiconductor
all_entries.whyCore: Manufactures every leading-edge AI chip — NVDA, AVGO, AMD all fab here. Absolute monopoly on 3nm/2nm nodes. No AI hardware stack exists without TSMC.
all_entries.moat: Technical monopoly. 3-5 year lead on any competitor. Cannot be replicated.
all_entries.riskNote: Taiwan geopolitical risk. But if this risk materializes, entire AI hardware thesis collapses regardless.

all_entries.name: AppLovin
all_entries.whyCore: AXON 2.0 AI ad targeting model outperforms Google and Meta networks for mobile and e-commerce. Revenue growing 70%+ YoY. Biggest gap in current watchlist — no AI advertising exposure anywhere in portfolio.
all_entries.moat: Network effects in ad data. The more advertisers use it, the better the model gets. Classic flywheel.
all_entries.riskNote: High valuation. Cyclical ad spend exposure. Regulatory scrutiny of ad tech.

all_entries.name: ASML Holding
all_entries.whyCore: The only manufacturer of EUV lithography machines. TSMC, Samsung, Intel all buy exclusively from ASML. No leading-edge chip fab operates without it. The chokepoint above the chokepoint.
all_entries.moat: Absolute monopoly. 20+ years to replicate. Proprietary technology developed over decades.
all_entries.riskNote: Semiconductor capex cycle (equipment orders front-run then slow). China export restrictions reducing TAM.

all_entries.name: Vistra Energy
all_entries.whyCore: Nuclear + natural gas generation. Direct complement to CEG position. AI data centers need reliable 24/7 baseload power. VST has nuclear assets that cannot be replicated and has historically matched or exceeded CEG returns.
all_entries.moat: Nuclear plants are irreplaceable 40-year assets. Cannot build new ones quickly. AI data center demand creates structural pricing power.
all_entries.riskNote: Commodity exposure (gas generation). Regulatory environment. More leverage than CEG.

all_entries.name: ServiceNow
all_entries.whyCore: Enterprise workflow automation platform. >98% revenue retention — once a company runs 500 workflows on NOW, they never leave. AI is accelerating adoption as companies embed LLMs into their workflow automation.
all_entries.moat: Extreme switching costs. Workflow processes become deeply embedded in company operations.
all_entries.riskNote: High valuation (P/E 55x). Competition from Microsoft Copilot embedded in Office. Rate sensitivity on long-duration growth.

all_entries.name: Datadog
all_entries.whyCore: Cloud and AI infrastructure observability. Q1 2026: revenue $1.01B (+32% YoY) — first time crossing $1B. FCF $289M at 29% margin. Full-year guide raised by $240M at midpoint to $4.3-4.34B. Four consecutive quarters of accelerating growth. AI workloads generate more observability data, not less — DDOG benefits from AI proliferation.
all_entries.moat: Platform stickiness. Observability data is deeply integrated into operations. Net revenue retention >120%.
all_entries.riskNote: Software rotation headwind in short term. Cloud providers building native monitoring. Street-high target $320.

all_entries.name: Applied Materials
all_entries.whyCore: Largest semiconductor equipment company. Makes deposition, etch, and inspection tools for every chip fab. Every new fab (TSMC Arizona, Intel Ohio) orders billions in AMAT equipment. Geopolitical onshoring creates a multi-year supercycle.
all_entries.moat: Duopoly with Lam Research. Deep customer integration — switching equipment mid-process is impractical.
all_entries.riskNote: Semiconductor capex cycles. China export restrictions. More cyclical than ASML.

all_entries.name: Broadcom
all_entries.whyCore: Custom ASIC design for hyperscalers + AI networking silicon. Q2 FY2026 (June 3): revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY, beat), AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guided $29.4B total, AI revenue >200% YoY. Order book >$70B from Google, Meta, Apple. Stock dipped -3% post-earnings on minor infrastructure software miss — the AI hardware acceleration is confirmed and accelerating.
all_entries.moat: Deep hyperscaler relationships. Custom ASIC design is irreplaceable once embedded — hyperscalers design multi-year chip roadmaps around AVGO.
all_entries.riskNote: Infrastructure software segment dragging (caused post-earnings dip). Semiconductor cycle exposure if hyperscaler capex decelerates.
all_entries.activeSignal: Post-Q2 dip is the entry window. Q3 guided $29.4B (+84% YoY) with AI revenue >200%. Hard data does not support the sell-off narrative.

all_entries.name: NVIDIA
all_entries.whyCore: Center of the AI accelerator market. H100/H200/B100 GPU demand remains backlogged. Data center revenue growing 400%+ YoY. The consensus pick — but the fundamentals justify it.
all_entries.moat: CUDA software ecosystem. 10+ year developer lock-in. No competitor has matched the full hardware + software stack.
all_entries.riskNote: Export controls risk. Valuation premium requires sustained AI capex. Consensus = less asymmetry.

all_entries.name: Eaton
all_entries.whyCore: Electrical components, power management, data center power infrastructure. Less visible than nuclear plays but more durable — every data center, every EV charger, every grid upgrade uses Eaton components.
all_entries.moat: Industrial switching costs. Long-term utility and data center supply relationships.
all_entries.riskNote: Industrial cyclicality. Premium valuation for the sector.

all_entries.name: Palantir
all_entries.whyCore: Government AI deployment moat. AIP platform adopted by US military and intelligence. Q1 2026 revenue +85% YoY, FCF $925M at 57% margin. Business is genuinely exceptional — the problem is valuation.
all_entries.moat: Government contract stickiness. Security clearance requirements create near-impossible switching.
all_entries.riskNote: P/S ~80x trailing. P/E 215x+. CEO Karp sold $65.9M open-market Feb 2026. Jefferies $70 target (vs ~$132). 40% revenue from US government — sequestration risk. International commercial grew only 2% FY2025. Does NOT pass capital preservation filter.

all_entries.name: Nextpower
all_entries.whyCore: Solar tracker hardware and software for utility-scale solar. Rebranded from Nextracker to Nextpower in Nov 2025 to reflect evolution into integrated power technology platform. FY2026 revenue $3.56B (+20% YoY), FCF $514M, record backlog $5.25B.
all_entries.moat: Software-enabled hardware — tracking optimization creates switching costs.
all_entries.riskNote: Solar cyclicality. Tariff impact on gross margins (recovering). Policy risk under current administration.
all_entries.activeSignal: Record $5.25B backlog with tariff headwind absorbing — gross margin recovery trajectory is the key proof point.

all_entries.name: Vertiv
all_entries.whyCore: Data center power and cooling infrastructure. Every AI GPU cluster generates massive heat. Vertiv makes the cooling systems that keep them running.
all_entries.moat: Deep data center integration — cooling systems are designed into facilities, not swapped.
all_entries.riskNote: Valuation elevated. Theme crowding. Industrial cyclicality.

all_entries.name: Alphabet
all_entries.whyCore: Search monopoly + YouTube + Google Cloud (GCP) + DeepMind AI. Diversified quality compounder with AI optionality. Trading at a discount to Microsoft and Meta on forward earnings.
all_entries.moat: Search network effects + advertising data flywheel + YouTube creator ecosystem.
all_entries.riskNote: Antitrust/regulatory pressure. Search disruption narrative from AI. AI capex margin drag.

all_entries.name: Cameco
all_entries.whyCore: Largest publicly traded uranium producer. Nuclear fuel supply chain exposure as reactor demand grows from data center electricity needs.
all_entries.moat: Tier-1 uranium mines with 20+ year reserves. Permitting for new mines takes decades.
all_entries.riskNote: Commodity cycle risk. Geopolitical mine risk (Kazakhstan). Narrative crowding.
all_entries.activeSignal: Down -8.61% — sharp sector dislocation alongside other nuclear names.

all_entries.name: GE Vernova
all_entries.whyCore: Gas turbines, wind, grid modernization equipment. Direct beneficiary of grid capacity shortage as AI data centers require more electricity.
all_entries.moat: Long-term service agreements on turbines. Fleet installed base creates recurring revenue.
all_entries.riskNote: Industrial cyclicality. Execution on margin improvement. Theme crowding after power narrative enthusiasm.

all_entries.name: Quanta Services
all_entries.whyCore: Physical grid construction — high-voltage transmission lines, substations, data center electrical work. Every grid upgrade, every new data center, every renewable farm needs Quanta's crews.
all_entries.moat: Skilled labor scarcity creates barrier to entry. Long-term utility framework agreements.
all_entries.riskNote: Project execution. Utility capex timing. Rate-sensitive valuation.

all_entries.name: Reddit
all_entries.whyCore: Human-generated conversation data as scarce AI training input. Data licensing deals with Google and others. Ad monetization improving.
all_entries.moat: Community network effects. Authentic human discourse increasingly scarce for AI training.
all_entries.riskNote: Post-IPO volatility. Platform governance risk. Ad-cycle sensitivity.

all_entries.name: TransMedics
all_entries.whyCore: Organ transplant preservation and logistics network. OCS (Organ Care System) dramatically expands usable donor organ pool. Network effects as more transplant centers use the platform.
all_entries.moat: Transplant center relationships + regulatory approvals + network effects.
all_entries.riskNote: Execution volatility. Healthcare reimbursement risk. Small-cap valuation compression.

all_entries.name: BWX Technologies
all_entries.whyCore: Only US manufacturer of naval nuclear reactor components — legally mandated by US Navy for all submarines and aircraft carriers. Q1 2026: $860M revenue, FY guided $3.75B+. Backlog $8.65B (+77% YoY) = 2.3x annual revenue already contracted. $1.29B naval nuclear award + $165M Ford-class reactor contract. 60% converts to revenue by end of 2027.
all_entries.moat: Legal monopoly. Federal law requires US Navy nuclear propulsion components from BWXT — no substitute, no foreign alternative permitted. Zero AI disruption risk. Not a speculative SMR play — mandated defense infrastructure with revenue pre-sold.
all_entries.riskNote: Defense budget sequestration >20% would reduce naval nuclear orders. SMR commercial timeline uncertain — not in base case. Stock is fully valued if growth stalls.
all_entries.earlyEntrySignal: Market prices BWXT as a cyclical industrial compounder. Correct read: legally mandated defense utility with 2.3x annual revenue in contracted backlog already signed. The $8.65B is not speculative — it is executed contracts. SMR upside is free optionality on top. The narrative says nuclear is speculative; the data says this specific company has revenue pre-sold through 2027.
all_entries.catalystWindow: Additional naval nuclear contract awards throughout 2026. Q2 and Q3 2026 earnings confirming 60%+ backlog-to-revenue conversion.
all_entries.invalidation: Defense budget cut >20% to naval nuclear programs. Quarterly revenue falls below $870M for two consecutive quarters — signals backlog conversion is slowing.

all_entries.name: Energy Transfer LP
all_entries.whyCore: Midstream pipeline operator with $25B in firm-fee AI data center gas contracts (Oracle, hyperscalers) at 18-year average duration. Q1 2026: DCF +17%. 7-8% LP distribution yield — paid to hold while AI data center buildout accelerates. Not in the AI narrative, but directly contractually tied to AI infrastructure demand.
all_entries.moat: 90,000+ miles of physical pipeline infrastructure that cannot be replicated. Contracted fee revenue — payment is not contingent on gas prices but on gas volumes flowing through the pipe. AI data centers cannot run without natural gas; ET owns the pipes.
all_entries.riskNote: MLP structure — K-1 tax form, cannot be held in some account types (IRA restrictions vary). High leverage ($55B+ debt). Distribution could be cut if AI data center buildout slows significantly.
all_entries.earlyEntrySignal: Pipeline companies are entirely outside the AI investment narrative. The connection is simple: AI data centers consume enormous natural gas for power generation; natural gas travels by pipeline; ET has $25B in contractual fee revenue tied to this demand over 18 years. The market classifies ET as an energy stock — it is actually an AI infrastructure income vehicle. 7-8% yield provides income while waiting for the narrative to catch up.
all_entries.catalystWindow: Q2 2026 earnings: confirmation of AI data center contract revenue ramp. Any new major hyperscaler gas supply contract announced publicly. Natural gas policy tailwinds from AI energy demand narrative.
all_entries.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex cut >20% reduces gas demand growth. Distribution cut — signals cash flow stress. Two consecutive quarters of DCF decline QoQ.

all_entries.name: Kinsale Capital
all_entries.whyCore: Specialty excess & surplus (E&S) lines insurer with 77.4% combined ratio — industry-best by 15-20 percentage points vs. sector average of 95-100%. Q1 2026: EPS +38%, net written premiums +11%. Down 24% from 52-week high on E&S market softening narrative. Underwriting discipline is structurally intact — the dislocation is narrative, not fundamental.
all_entries.moat: Proprietary actuarial data and technology-first underwriting culture built over 15+ years of loss history. 77.4% combined ratio cannot be replicated by a new entrant — it requires 15 years of data discipline. Low expense ratio from technology-driven operations. The moat compounds every year the database grows.
all_entries.riskNote: E&S market softening reduces premium pricing power as competitors enter. Catastrophic loss year (hurricane, wildfire season) could spike combined ratio above 85%. Insurance is narrative-unfavorable in an AI bull market — nobody writes about it.
all_entries.earlyEntrySignal: Nobody talks about insurance in an AI bull market. KNSL is down 24% with no deterioration in underwriting quality — the dislocation is narrative, not data. This is precisely the Buffett entry template: boring business, down for sentiment reasons, best-in-class economics compounding at 15%+ for a decade, high FCF, no AI disruption risk. The crowd will never arrive here during an AI bull market — which is exactly when you buy it.
all_entries.catalystWindow: Q2 2026 earnings: combined ratio confirmation below 80% proves underwriting quality intact. E&S market pricing stabilization data from industry reports.
all_entries.invalidation: Combined ratio rises above 85% for two consecutive quarters — signals competitive pressure has eroded underwriting standards. Premium growth falls below 0% YoY — structural market share loss.

all_entries.name: Celestica
all_entries.whyCore: Contract electronics manufacturer with ~80% revenue from hyperscaler AI infrastructure (AI servers, networking switches, storage). Q1 2026: +53% revenue YoY, FY guide raised to $19B. Same AI infrastructure demand tailwind as AVGO and NVDA — trades at a fraction of the multiple with minimal institutional awareness. FCF positive. Profitable. Pre-consensus.
all_entries.moat: Deep hyperscaler manufacturing integration — multi-year supply agreements with design-to-manufacturing integration create switching costs. CLS is embedded in hardware design cycles, not just assembly. Specialized clean-room manufacturing capability.
all_entries.riskNote: Customer concentration — top 2-3 hyperscalers represent majority of revenue. Low gross margins (contract manufacturing model — revenue is large, margins are thin). Trade and tariff exposure on manufacturing inputs.
all_entries.earlyEntrySignal: Druckenmiller-style early entry: same AI infrastructure demand as AVGO, but no major fund showed significant new CLS position in Q1 2026 13F filings. +53% revenue growth with $19B FY guide raised — the crowd hasn't arrived. Trades at 18x forward PE while AVGO trades at 32x for the same demand tailwind. The mispricing exists because CLS is classified as a boring contract manufacturer, not an AI infrastructure name.
all_entries.catalystWindow: Q2 2026 earnings: FY guide raised further above $19B confirms AI hardware demand acceleration. Major analyst initiation at bulge-bracket bank would force institutional recognition.
all_entries.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex cut >15%. Loss of a top-2 customer relationship disclosed in earnings. Revenue growth drops below 20% for two consecutive quarters — signals the AI hardware manufacturing boom is plateauing.

all_entries.name: GE Aerospace
all_entries.whyCore: Pure-play aerospace engine manufacturer after GE's industrial breakup. FY2026: $211B backlog, $8-8.4B FCF guided. LEAP engine is the monopoly platform on narrow-body jets (Boeing 737 MAX, Airbus A320neo). Commercial aviation demand exceeds manufacturing capacity — airlines cannot get planes fast enough. Defense engine contracts (F-35, military rotorcraft) provide parallel revenue.
all_entries.moat: Jet engine technology requires 20+ years to develop and another decade for FAA certification. The LEAP engine backlog represents 10+ years of contracted production. Service revenue on the installed engine fleet (aftermarket parts + maintenance) is recurring and long-duration — every engine sold creates a service annuity.
all_entries.riskNote: Premium valuation (42x forward PE) leaves limited margin of safety — requires execution on backlog delivery. Boeing production bottlenecks constrain engine deliveries (GE engines need Boeing planes to install into). Labor/strike risk at key manufacturing facilities.
all_entries.earlyEntrySignal: Aviation supercycle is undervalued relative to AI capex narrative — institutional attention is entirely on semiconductors. The $211B backlog is not speculative: airlines have paid deposits on planes that require GE engines. The narrative says 'industrials already ran' — the data says backlog keeps growing every quarter. Entry requires a pullback to ~35x forward PE or earnings-driven dislocation.
all_entries.catalystWindow: Q2 2026 earnings: backlog growth confirmation + FCF trajectory update. Boeing production ramp — any acceleration in Boeing 737 MAX deliveries directly improves GE engine delivery cadence and revenue recognition.
all_entries.invalidation: Backlog declines for two consecutive quarters — demand saturation signal. FCF misses FY guidance by >15%. Boeing production rate cut that directly reduces LEAP engine orders in the near term.
